meta.description: Deterministic IPA->Latin mapping for the SPANISH Peshitta corpus.
meta.note: transliterate_es.py is authoritative; this file mirrors its hard-coded tables. SCHOLARLY and PRETTY are language-neutral and byte-for-byte identical to the English Peshitta; HISPANICIZED is a Spanish reader-aid.

segmentation: Split on ASCII space ' ' only; spaces are preserved verbatim.
Within each token, left-to-right longest match:
2. vowel (a ɑ e i o u) + \u02D0 (ː) -> long-vowel segment (2 chars)
word-initial = the first segment of a token (used by the Alaph rule).

A token boundary is whitespace OR a hyphen, so the segment right after a hyphen is also word-initial. This hyphen-boundary semantics is applied for the HISPANICIZED tier only (e.g. 'bar-ʔaba' -> 'bar-aba', the post-hyphen Alaph being word-initial and dropped). SCHOLARLY and PRETTY keep the English transducer's space-only semantics so they stay byte-for-byte identical to the English Peshitta (scholarly Alaph is always 'ʾ'; pretty keeps the medial apostrophe, e.g. 'bar-'aba').

alaph.pretty: word-initial -> '' (drop); else -> "'". word-initial = first segment of a SPACE-delimited token (hyphen is NOT a boundary here, matching English).
alaph.hispanicized: word-initial -> '' (drop); else -> "'". word-initial = first segment of a whitespace- OR hyphen-delimited token, so a post-hyphen Alaph (e.g. 'bar-ʔaba') is word-initial => dropped ('bar-aba').

hard_g.applies_to: ɡ (ɡ) and ɣ (ɣ), hispanicized tier only
hard_g.rule: render as 'gu' when the NEXT segment is a front vowel (/e/, /eː/, /i/, /iː/); otherwise 'g'. Spanish hard-g rule avoiding g -> /x/ before e/i.

rules.unmapped: Any char not covered above (besides space) is recorded and passed through unchanged.

documented_limitations: Spanish lacks /h ħ z v ʃ ʔ ʕ/ and the emphatics, so they are approximated or collapsed: back fricatives ħ & x -> 'j' (/x/); soft ð & ɣ collapse onto Spanish's own intervocalic d/g spirantization; /z/ -> 's' (voicing lost); θ -> 'z' yields Castilian /θ/ but Latin-American /s/; Spanish silent 'h' means the /h/ (he) letter is usually unpronounced; ayin and word-initial alaph are dropped.
